A new comment in CIVIL ENG - GO TG

automationCandidates.description: Style, season, BOM, sample, and program-development coordination.
Unclassified email that still may need summary or manual routing.
Shipment updates, AWB details, pickup confirmations, delivery timing, and freight coordination.
Final inspection planning, pass/fail status, quality findings, and compliance follow-up.
Invoices, POs, LC topics, reports, and commercial document exchange.
Daily production, shipment pending, wash balance, and execution-status reporting.
Buyer or merchandising requests about cost sheets, pricing, target costs, or non-e-bid commercial discussions.
Comments, notifications, and collaboration artifacts that usually need routing more than drafting.
ERP, PO, and internal approval requests that require internal ownership or authorization.

observations: This inbox behaves like buyer, merchandising, and export-operations coordination rather than end-customer support.
The strongest automation opening is human-reviewed draft generation, not full auto-send.
Category volume clusters around development, logistics, quality, and commercial document exchange.
Gap and inspection partners dominate the external footprint, which makes the workflow legible enough for a focused AI pilot.
Internal approvals and ambiguous threads should remain manual even in the demo story.
